evidence.correctness_and_freshness: The task asks specifically for @solana/web3.js v2 (createSolanaRpc). The backend uses Python solana-py (not @solana/web3.js at all), and the React v2 example uses fabricated APIs: ConnectorProvider/ArcProvider/useTransferSOL from @solana/kit do not exist as documented. react@^19.2.7 is an invalid/nonexistent version. Core v2 functional API createSolanaRpc is mentioned but never actually used.
evidence.executability: Python backend code is mostly self-contained but the CORS middleware snippet is a separate ```python block referencing 'app' from main.py — extractor would write it as a second file or duplicate. The v2 React example imports symbols that don't exist (useTransferSOL, ArcProvider, ConnectorProvider from @solana/kit), causing import failures. react@^19.2.7 npm install would fail. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo).
evidence.completeness: (a) RPC provider signup URLs present; (b) installs in bash blocks; (c) .env.example present; (d) backend code present; (e) frontend code present; (f) test commands present but pytest path 'backend.main' requires a package layout not specified. Multiple conflicting React samples (server-signed vs wallet-signed) could confuse extractor. Env vars listed match those referenced.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Mix of official (fastapi.tiangolo.com, create-react-app.dev) and third-party (helius, smashingmagazine, gitguardian). No canonical @solana/web3.js v2 docs URL (solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js or docs.solana.com) cited near the v2 code. One likely fabricated URL: solana.com/docs/tools/commerce-kit/quickstart/react-hooks.

rubric_notes: Playbook sidesteps the actual requirement (@solana/web3.js v2 in JS) by implementing the transfer in Python solana-py. The v2 React example uses fabricated/hallucinated package exports (useTransferSOL from @solana/kit, ArcProvider, ConnectorProvider). docs_unavailable=true; long-tail handling applied but internal inconsistency (claims v2 ecosystem but never calls createSolanaRpc) lowers correctness.
